article_title: SoftBank to Build 10 GW Data Center at Former Ohio Uranium Enrichment Site With $33.3 Billion in Japanese Funding

content_preview.title: SoftBank to Build 10 GW Data Center at Former Ohio Uranium Enrichment Site With $33.3 Billion in Japanese Funding
content_preview.summary: The U.S. Department of Energy announced a partnership with SoftBank subsidiary SB Energy to develop a 10 GW data center and matching power generation complex at the decommissioned Portsmouth uranium enrichment plant in Pike County, Ohio, backed by $33.3 billion in Japanese investment.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The U.S. Department of Energy announced on March 20, 2026, a public-private partnership with SB Energy, a SoftBank Group subsidiary, to build a [10 gigawatt data center campus](https://www.energy.gov/articles/energy-department-announces-partnership-ensure-affordable-energy-and-power-americas-ai) at the decommissioned Portsmouth Gaseous Diffusion Plant in Pike County, Ohio. The project, rebranded as the PORTS Technology Campus, would transform a Cold War-era uranium enrichment facili...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured article covering the DOE-SoftBank partnership with clear sections on power generation, economics, site history, and context. 533 words appropriate for News.
editor_notes.source_verification: DOE announcement confirmed: 10 GW data center, SB Energy, Portsmouth site, Pike County, PORTS Technology Campus, Chris Wright. DOE fact sheet confirmed: $33.3B Japanese funding, 9.2 GW natural gas, $4.2B transmission package, AEP Ohio, 765 kV lines, 10K construction jobs, 2K permanent jobs, $40M Community Benefits Agreement, land lease, cleanup. CBS confirmed: Chris Wright quote about winning AI race, Stargate connection.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core claims all verified. Minor issue: some contextual claims in the final paragraph ($700B hyperscaler capex, Japan $550B total US investment, Texas/Michigan campuses, Meta Indiana 1 GW comparison) are attributed via inline links to CBS but were not found in that specific article. These are accurate industry facts from general reporting but loosely attributed. Not severe enough to warrant changes as the core story is fully sourced.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and factual. Presents the project details without editorializing. Appropriately frames the DOE's 'net benefit' framing as the DOE's characterization rather than the article's own assessment.
editor_notes.originality: No prior SoftBank Ohio data center coverage. Novel topic.
concerns: Some contextual claims in the final paragraph attributed to CBS were not found in that specific article, though they are accurate industry knowledge
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong submission with core claims verified against primary DOE sources. Minor attribution looseness in contextual paragraph does not affect the article's factual integrity. Approved.
